    <h1 {...{
      "id": "network-operations"
    }}>{`Network Operations`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to connect your UXP plugin to the web`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP provides powerful network APIs that let your plugin `}<strong parentName="p">{`fetch data`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`access online services`}</strong>{`, and `}<strong parentName="p">{`communicate in real time`}</strong>{`—all while keeping users safe through a robust permission model. This guide covers how to use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`XMLHttpRequest`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`WebSocket`}</inlineCode>{` in UXP, how to declare network permissions properly, and how to handle errors.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "system-requirements"
    }}>{`System requirements`}</h2>
    <p>{`Please make make sure your development environment uses the following `}<strong parentName="p">{`minimum versions`}</strong>{` to avoid compatibility issues:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest v5`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "network-security"
    }}>{`Network Security`}</h2>
    <p>{`By default, UXP plugins can't connect to the internet; this helps keep users safe by preventing unwanted or hidden network activity. If your plugin needs to fetch data, load images, or use WebSockets, `}<strong parentName="p">{`you must declare`}</strong>{` the right `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
      }}>{`network permissions`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`In your plugin's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`, use the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#networkpermission"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`requiredPermissions.network`}</inlineCode></a>{` object to lists the domains your plugin can access.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "network": {
      "domains": [
        "https://api.example.com",
        "https://*.adobe.io"
      ]
    }
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`You may use wildcards for domain patterns, such as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"https://api.*.example.com"`}</inlineCode>{` to match multiple environments (for example, dev, staging, and production). `}<strong parentName="p">{`Any request to an unlisted domain will fail`}</strong>{` with a permission error.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`All APIs support both HTTP and HTTPS, but macOS restricts `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`http://`}</inlineCode>{` for security reasons. You should use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`https://`}</inlineCode>{` instead.`}</p>

      "id": "choose-the-right-network-api"

    <p>{`Network APIs are available globally in UXP, you don't need to import them. Let's explore each one in depth.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-fetch"
    }}>{`Using fetch()`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/Data%20Transfers/fetch.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode></a>{` API is the simplest and most flexible way to make network requests. It's modern, asynchronous, and built into the UXP global scope—no `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` needed.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Get weather forecast for San Jose ☀️
async function getForecast() {
try {
const response = await fetch(
  "https://api.weather.gov/gridpoints/MTR/99,82/forecast"
  );

  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error(
      \`HTTP error \${response.status}: \${response.statusText}\`
    );
  }
  const data = await response.json(); // 👈 parse the response as JSON
  console.log(
    \`Forecast: \${data.properties.periods[0].detailedForecast}\`
  );

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("❌ Failed to fetch forecast:", err);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "network": {
      "domains": ["https://api.weather.gov"]
    }
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Remember to parse the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`response`}</inlineCode>{` object as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`json()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`text()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`blob()`}</inlineCode>{` for the appropriate data types. These methods return `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise"
      }}>{`Promises`}</a>{`, so you must use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`await`}</inlineCode>{` to get the data.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-fetching-an-image-dynamically"
    }}>{`Example: Fetching an Image Dynamically`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can even use network data directly in your UI.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="HTML, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>

`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-1"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...

    "network": {
      "domains": ["https://picsum.photos/"]

}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<img>`}</inlineCode>{` tag works seamlessly in UXP, provided the remote domain is allow-listed.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-post-requests-and-json-payloads"
    }}>{`Example: POST Requests and JSON Payloads`}</h3>
    <p>{`Many web APIs expect `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Methods/POST"
      }}>{`POST requests`}</a>{` with JSON bodies. Here's how to send data safely.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`async function postUserData(user) {
  try {
    const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/users", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify(user)
    });

    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(\`Server error: \${response.status}\`);
    const result = await response.json();
    console.log("✅ User created:", result);

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed to post user data:", err);
  }
}

// Example usage
postUserData({ name: "Jamie", role: "Editor" });
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`UXP is not a browser environment`}</p>
    <p>{`We have `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../introduction/index.md#uxp-is-not-a-browser"
      }}>{`mentioned this before`}</a>{`; some features you're familiar with are not available. For example, you can't use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`TextDecoder`}</inlineCode>{` class to read a data stream.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`async function fetchStreamedData() {
  const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/stream");
  const reader = response.body.getReader();
  const decoder = new TextDecoder(); // ❌ decoder is undefined here!!
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-xmlhttprequest-xhr"
    }}>{`Using XMLHttpRequest (XHR)`}</h3>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`XMLHttpRequest`}</inlineCode>{` is the older, event-driven API. It's still useful when you need progress events or upload monitoring.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`function getForecastWithXHR() {
const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
xhr.open(
  "GET",
  "https://api.weather.gov/gridpoints/MTR/99,82/forecast"
);
xhr.responseType = "json";

xhr.onload = () => {
  if (xhr.status === 200) {
    console.log(
      \`Forecast: \${xhr.response.properties.periods[0].detailedForecast}\`
    );
  } else {
    console.error(\`XHR failed with status \${xhr.status}\`);
  }
};

  xhr.onerror = () => console.error("Network error occurred");
  xhr.send();
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{

}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-websocket"
    }}>{`Using WebSocket`}</h3>
    <p>{`WebSockets let your plugin maintain a live connection to a server—ideal for apps that need to update in real time.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-2"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`let socket;

async function connectToServer() {
  try {
    if (socket) {
      console.log("🔌 Disconnecting existing socket...");
      socket.close();
      socket = null;
      return;
    }

    socket = new WebSocket(
      "wss://javascript.info/article/websocket/demo/hello"
    );

    socket.onopen = () => {
      console.log("✅ WebSocket connection established");
      socket.send("Hello from Premiere plugin!");
    };

    socket.onmessage = (event) => {
      console.log(\`📩 Message from server: \${event.data}\`);
    };

    socket.onerror = (err) => {
      console.error("⚠️ WebSocket error:", err);
    };

    socket.onclose = () => {
      console.log("Connection closed");
      socket = null;
    };

  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Failed to connect via WebSocket:", e);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-3"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "network": {
     "domains": ["wss://javascript.info"]
    }
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header,text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`UXP supports WebSocket clients only`}</p>
    <p>{`Plugins can connect to WebSocket servers, but cannot host or accept incoming connections.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "handling-errors-and-timeouts"
    }}>{`Handling Errors and Timeouts`}</h3>
    <p>{`Network calls can fail—the user may be offline, the endpoint may be down, or your permission list might be incomplete.`}</p>
    <p>{`Best practices:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Always wrap network calls in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`try...catch`}</inlineCode>{` blocks.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`response.ok`}</inlineCode>{` to detect HTTP errors.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Set reasonable timeouts for long operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Log informative errors using `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console.error()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`async function safeFetch(url, options = {}, timeoutMs = 8000) {
  const controller = new AbortController();
  const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);

  try {
    const res = await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`HTTP \${res.status}\`);
    return await res.json();
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Network request failed:", err);
    throw err;
  } finally {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Network Security Model`}</strong>{`: By default, UXP plugins cannot access the internet. All network operations require explicit declaration of permitted domains in the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file under `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`requiredPermissions`}</inlineCode>{` to prevent unwanted network activity.`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Add specific domains to the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`domains`}</inlineCode>{` array (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"https://api.example.com"`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Use wildcards for flexible domain patterns (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"https://*.adobe.io"`}</inlineCode>{`),`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Any request to an unlisted domain will fail with a permission error,`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`On macOS, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`http://`}</inlineCode>{` URLs are restricted for security reasons; use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`https://`}</inlineCode>{` instead.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Three Network APIs`}</strong>{`: Choose based on your use case:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Modern, promise-based API for HTTP requests; supports JSON, text, binary data, and streaming; ideal for most use cases.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`XMLHttpRequest`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Legacy, event-driven API; useful for progress events and upload tracking; provides compatibility with older code patterns.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`WebSocket`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Real-time, bidirectional communication; maintains persistent connections to servers; client-only (plugins cannot host WebSocket servers).`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ol>
